abstract: Initializes a rule with the specified predicate.
overview:
## Return Value

A new predicate-based rule object.

## Discussion

Rules based on [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSPredicate] objects typically test information in the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary of the rule system evaluating the rule. For example, the following code creates a rule you might use to determine whether an enemy character in a game behaves aggressively.

This example presumes the rule system’s state dictionary contains an object for the key `player`, which in turn exposes a numeric value for the key `health`. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule] class by itself does nothing in its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/performAction(in:)] method—to create actions for predicate-based rules, you must subclass [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule].

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

# init(predicate:)

**Initializer**

Initializes a rule with the specified predicate.

## Declaration

```swift
init(predicate: NSPredicate)
```

## Parameters

- **predicate**: A predicate to be tested when evaluating the rule.

## Return Value

A new predicate-based rule object.

## Discussion

Rules based on [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSPredicate] objects typically test information in the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/state] dictionary of the rule system evaluating the rule. For example, the following code creates a rule you might use to determine whether an enemy character in a game behaves aggressively.



This example presumes the rule system’s state dictionary contains an object for the key `player`, which in turn exposes a numeric value for the key `health`. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule] class by itself does nothing in its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/performAction(in:)] method—to create actions for predicate-based rules, you must subclass [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKNSPredicateRule].

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].
